_comment: Place this at <NAS volume>/config.json (the path mapped to /data). Use the 'persons' array for one or more people (whole family in one config). For a single person you can use a single 'person' object instead. Lines starting with _ are comments — real JSON has no comments, so this file IS valid JSON; just edit the values.

capsolver._comment: Your CapSolver key — shared across all persons. Required on a headless NAS for CAPTCHA-protected brokers (no manual fallback possible).

accounts._comment: Optional shared site logins. Per-person opt-out usually doesn't need these — leave blank unless a broker requires an account.

notify._comment: iMessage (textTo) is macOS-only — leave blank on a NAS. Pick ONE of the options below (delete the others). One summary is sent per run.
notify._option_discord: Discord: copy the channel webhook URL and APPEND /slack to it (Discord rejects the plain payload on its native endpoint). e.g. https://discord.com/api/webhooks/<ID>/<TOKEN>/slack
notify._option_ntfy: ntfy (free): set webhook to https://ntfy.sh/<a-topic-you-invent-yourself> — anyone who knows the topic can read it, so make it long and random. Install the ntfy app and subscribe to the same topic. Self-host later by swapping the host.
notify._option_telegram: Telegram: create a bot via @BotFather (get botToken), message it once, then get chatId from https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates. Use the 'telegram' block instead of 'webhook'.
notify._telegram_note: To use Telegram instead, blank out 'webhook' above and fill this in:

email._comment: Optional SMTP for email-based opt-outs. One sending mailbox sends on behalf of everyone. Gmail needs an App Password.
